pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 12-13: PROJECT_NAME="GitHubBrowser", PROJECT_DIR="/home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/$PROJECT_NAME". Script creates this directory and opens it in Android Studio.

setup_task.sh line 22 creates base package directory. Export_result.sh line 7 constructs MODEL_DIR as "$PROJECT_DIR/app/src/main/java/com/example/githubbrowser/model" and looks for Owner.kt and GitHubRepo.kt in this directory. Task description specifies models should be created in 'com.example.githubbrowser.model' package.

task.json description explicitly specifies creating two data classes: 'Create a data class Owner' and 'Create a data class GitHubRepo'. Export_result.sh lines 19-24 check for these specific files. Verifier.py lines 65 and 93 verify their existence and content.

task.json description (line 5) specifies 'Add the org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-serialization-json:1.6.3 dependency to the app module.' Verified via Maven Central Repository that version 1.6.3 exists (published 2024-02-16). The metadata correctly identifies the artifact ID 'kotlinx-serialization-json'; the full coordinate includes group ID 'org.jetbrains.kotlinx' and version '1.6.3'. Verifier.py line 56 checks for this dependency using regex pattern matching the full artifact ID.

task.json description (line 5) specifies 'Add the Kotlin Serialization plugin (org.jetbrains.kotlin.plugin.serialization) to the project.' Verified via Gradle Plugin Registry that org.jetbrains.kotlin.plugin.serialization is the official Kotlin serialization compiler plugin. Verifier.py line 44 checks for this plugin in build.gradle files.

setup_task.sh line 50 specifies 'id("org.jetbrains.kotlin.android") version "1.9.20"' in the root build.gradle.kts. This Kotlin version is compatible with the serialization plugin and supports @Serializable annotation.
setup_task.sh line 49 specifies 'id("com.android.application") version "8.2.0"' in the root build.gradle.kts.
setup_task.sh line 68 specifies 'targetSdk = 34' in the app/build.gradle.kts configuration.
setup_task.sh line 63 specifies 'compileSdk = 34' in the app/build.gradle.kts configuration.

pi_items.source: task.json description section 'Create Data Models' specifies exact Owner class structure. These fields correspond to actual GitHub API user object fields verified via GitHub REST API documentation.

Must be annotated with @Serializable with @SerialName mappings for snake_case JSON fields.

Verifier.py lines 132-137 check build_success flag which is determined by running 'assembleDebug' in export_result.sh line 56. Task description specifies 'build the project to ensure everything compiles successfully.'

privileged_info_summary: The add_kotlin_serialization task is a synthetic Android development task that requires integrating Kotlin Serialization into a Gradle-based Android project. The task creates a fresh Android project (GitHubBrowser) at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/GitHubBrowser using Android Gradle Plugin 8.2.0, Kotlin 1.9.20, Gradle 8.4, and compileSdk/targetSdk 34. The agent must: (1) apply the org.jetbrains.kotlin.plugin.serialization compiler plugin at project or app level, (2) add the org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-serialization-json:1.6.3 dependency to the app module, and (3) create two Kotlin data classes with @Serializable annotations in app/src/main/java/com/example/githubbrowser/model/: Owner.kt with 4 fields (id, login, avatarUrl, htmlUrl) and GitHubRepo.kt with 10 fields including an Owner reference, using @SerialName annotations to map JSON snake_case field names to Kotlin camelCase properties. The resulting project must compile successfully. All field names correspond to real GitHub API objects.
